title: Web: extract renderer-registry abstraction from DocumentViewer
description:
Refactor web/src/components/Document/DocumentViewer.tsx so the content renderer is chosen via a registry instead of hardcoded ReactMarkdown.

Steps:
1. Introduce DocumentRenderer type: { id; match(content, ref); Component }.
2. Create web/src/components/Document/renderers/index.ts with an ordered registry. First-match-wins; last entry is a catch-all MarkdownRenderer.
3. Extract the current ReactMarkdown body (code-block handler; syntax highlighting; search highlighting; plugins; the ReactMarkdown call itself) verbatim into renderers/MarkdownRenderer.tsx. Zero behavior change.
4. DocumentViewer.tsx becomes a thin shell: fetch content as today; keep header/footer/history-panel scaffolding; delegate the content body to the matched renderer.
5. Unit tests (Vitest) cover: registry picks HtmlRenderer for text/html; MarkdownRenderer for text/markdown; MarkdownRenderer fallback for unknown types.

Acceptance:
- Existing Markdown documents render byte-identically; visual regression check passes.
- Registry dispatch covered by unit tests.
- No direct ReactMarkdown import remains in DocumentViewer.tsx.

Part of story abfd6016. Unblocks task eeafaa78 (HtmlRenderer).
